content.full_text:
September 27, 1990

John Rothman, esq.,
Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
State of California

Dear Mr. Rothman,

per our conversation of September 26, 1990, I am sending you this facsimile letter to confirm the content of that conversation. It is my understanding that I asked you the following questions:

A. How does the federal court ruling voiding some provisions of proposition 73 affect the legality of contributions from one candidate's controlled campaign committee to another candidate's controlled committee?

B. Are there any additional restrictions in the law on such transfers?

C. When does the commission (FPPC) expect to receive a stay of the federal court decision, which stay would once again render such transfers illegal?

My understanding of your responses to these queries (respectively) is as follows:

A. The court held that the limitations in proposition 73 were unconstitutional because they were based on the fiscal year, and therefore discriminated against challengers in favor of incumbent candidates. As the prohibitions on transfers between candidates' committees depend upon the legality of the contribution limits, they were also invalidated by the ruling.

B. Transfers between candidates' committees are now legal in any amount due to the ruling, except that if the local agency in question imposes its own limits, then the discrimination cited above no longer exists, and such transfers are once again disallowed.

C. The FPPC is seeking a stay of the ruling in the interest of maintaining an orderly campaign process, and expects the question of the stay to be resolved within 24 to 48 hours of this conversation (June 26, 1990, 3 to 5 p.m.)

This is my understanding of the information we discussed. Please send confirmation by fax to Friends of Kevin Thurber, fax (408) 266-0229.

Sincerely,

Robert Gunter
